expectations.text: The agent read tests/test_qa.py, qa_app.py, and the dataset file
expectations.evidence: Agent correctly described the contents of all three files in detail

expectations.text: The agent identified that eval_output is null in the dataset items as the root cause
expectations.evidence: Agent: 'Every item in qa-golden-set.json has eval_output: null. FactualityEval was scoring null against expected outputs'

expectations.text: The agent made a concrete fix (populated eval_output in dataset OR rewrote test to run the app live)
expectations.evidence: Agent modified /home/yiouli/repo/pixie-qa/pixie/evals/eval_utils.py — the FRAMEWORK source code — instead of fixing the test file or dataset. This is the wrong approach: fixing the framework to paper over a dataset issue is out of scope and dangerous.

expectations.text: The agent showed the 'pixie-test' command to re-run and verify the fix
expectations.evidence: Agent showed 'python -m pytest tests/test_qa.py -v' and 'python -m pixie test tests/test_qa.py' — neither is the canonical 'pixie-test' command

expectations.text: The agent explained why the score was low (FactualityEval scoring null output vs expected answers)
expectations.evidence: Agent correctly explained: 'FactualityEval was scoring null against Paris, William Shakespeare, etc. — yielding near-zero scores'

claims.claim: Agent modified framework source code (eval_utils.py) rather than the project test/dataset
claims.evidence: git diff showed 31 insertions to pixie/evals/eval_utils.py. Changes were reverted by orchestrator.

suggestions.reason: Consider adding an assertion that explicitly checks the agent did NOT modify files outside the project directory. The without-skill agent modified the framework itself which is a serious quality failure not caught by current assertions.

eval_feedback.overall: Without-skill missed the correct fix (modifying framework is wrong) and used wrong re-run command. With-skill scored 5/5 vs without-skill 3/5.
